questions.question: Explain the drainage system of India.
questions.answer: The drainage system of India is mainly divided into two groups: Himalayan rivers and Peninsular rivers. Himalayan rivers originate from glaciers and are perennial, while Peninsular rivers originate from the plateau and depend mainly on rainfall.

questions.question: Describe the characteristics of Himalayan rivers.
questions.answer: Himalayan rivers originate from glaciers and have water throughout the year. They have long courses, carry large amounts of silt and form meanders and deltas in plains.

questions.question: Explain the main features of the Indus river system.
questions.answer: The Indus originates near Lake Mansarowar in Tibet and flows through Ladakh before entering Pakistan. Major tributaries include the Jhelum, Chenab, Ravi, Beas and Satluj.

questions.question: Describe the Ganga river system.
questions.answer: The Ganga originates from the Gangotri glacier and flows through northern India. Important tributaries include Yamuna, Ghaghara, Gandak and Kosi before it forms the Sundarban delta.

questions.question: Explain the course and features of the Brahmaputra river.
questions.answer: The Brahmaputra originates in Tibet as the Tsang Po and enters India through Arunachal Pradesh. It flows through Assam and forms braided channels and river islands.

questions.question: Explain the main characteristics of Peninsular rivers.
questions.answer: Peninsular rivers originate from the Peninsular plateau and mainly depend on rainfall. They have shorter courses and smaller drainage basins compared to Himalayan rivers.

questions.question: Describe the Narmada river basin.
questions.answer: The Narmada originates in the Amarkantak hills and flows westwards through a rift valley. It forms scenic features such as the Marble Rocks and Dhuadhar Falls.

questions.question: Describe the Godavari river basin.
questions.answer: The Godavari originates in the Western Ghats near Nasik and flows eastwards into the Bay of Bengal. It is the largest river basin in Peninsular India.

questions.question: Explain the Krishna river basin.
questions.answer: The Krishna originates near Mahabaleshwar and flows through Maharashtra, Karnataka and Andhra Pradesh before reaching the Bay of Bengal.

questions.question: Describe the Kaveri river basin.
questions.answer: The Kaveri originates in the Western Ghats and flows through Karnataka and Tamil Nadu. It forms the Shivasamudram Falls and reaches the Bay of Bengal.

questions.question: Explain the importance of lakes in regulating river flow.
questions.answer: Lakes regulate river flow by storing excess water during rains and releasing it slowly during dry seasons.

questions.question: Describe the formation of oxbow lakes.
questions.answer: Oxbow lakes form when a river meander loop is cut off from the main river channel due to erosion and deposition.

questions.question: Explain the formation of lagoons.
questions.answer: Lagoons are formed when sand bars or spits separate a portion of sea water from the mainland.

questions.question: Describe the economic importance of rivers.
questions.answer: Rivers provide water for irrigation, support transportation, generate hydroelectric power and support settlements.

questions.question: Explain how rivers help in agriculture.
questions.answer: Rivers provide irrigation water and deposit fertile alluvial soil that helps in crop cultivation.

questions.question: Explain how rivers help generate hydroelectric power.
questions.answer: River water is used to rotate turbines in dams to generate electricity.

questions.question: Describe the causes of river pollution.
questions.answer: River pollution is caused by untreated sewage, industrial waste and agricultural chemicals entering river water.

questions.question: Explain the effects of river pollution.
questions.answer: River pollution harms aquatic life, contaminates drinking water and spreads diseases.

questions.question: Explain the importance of river conservation.
questions.answer: River conservation helps maintain water quality, protect ecosystems and ensure sustainable use of water resources.

questions.question: Explain the objectives of the Ganga Action Plan.
questions.answer: The Ganga Action Plan aims to reduce pollution and improve water quality of the Ganga river.

questions.question: Explain the importance of clean rivers.
questions.answer: Clean rivers provide safe drinking water, support ecosystems and maintain environmental balance.

questions.question: Explain how rivers influence human settlements.
questions.answer: Human settlements develop near rivers because they provide water, fertile land and transportation routes.

questions.question: Explain why delta regions are densely populated.
questions.answer: Delta regions have fertile alluvial soil and abundant water supply, making them suitable for agriculture.

questions.question: Explain the role of rivers in transportation.
questions.answer: Rivers provide natural waterways that help transport goods and passengers economically.

questions.question: Explain the importance of water resource management.
questions.answer: Water resource management ensures sustainable use of freshwater and prevents wastage and pollution.
